prompt:
Goal: Create a realistic cricket player character sheet showing a male professional cricket player in a full uniform from three angles, like a sports-kit design preview.

Canvas: Wide horizontal studio composition, light gray seamless background, soft even lighting, high-resolution photorealistic 3D render or commercial sports photography look. Keep all three figures life-size and evenly spaced across the canvas.

Layout: Show exactly 3 full-body poses, each with a small black angle label centered below the feet: 1) left figure labeled "0°", front-facing, standing upright with a cricket bat resting vertically on the ground at his left side; 2) center figure labeled "45°", three-quarter front view, holding the bat diagonally upward across his body with both gloved hands; 3) right figure labeled "180°", back-facing, holding the bat vertically on the ground at his right side. The player’s face should be intentionally blurred or obscured in the front and three-quarter views.

Subject details: Athletic adult male with medium-brown skin, short black styled hair, clean sportswear silhouette. Outfit has exactly 7 visible equipment/clothing pieces: 1) red short-sleeve cricket jersey with black shoulders and gold trim, 2) navy cricket trousers, 3) white batting gloves, 4) gold leg pads, 5) white athletic cricket shoes, 6) pale wooden cricket bat with a bold red-and-black "SS" style logo, 7) white-and-red pad straps visible from the rear. Jersey front text reads QATAR AIRWAYS in large white letters, with a small gold crest on the left chest and subtle dark red patterned graphics across the lower torso. Jersey back is plain red with black side panels, gold-red gradient pattern near the waist, and a tiny gold mark below the collar.

Color and style: Use a premium cricket franchise kit aesthetic in red, black, and gold, navy pants, tan-gold protective pads, crisp white gloves and shoes, realistic fabric folds, glossy helmet-free presentation, and sharp product-render detail.

Constraints: Include exactly the 3 labeled angle views and no extra players, no stadium, no crowd, no watermark, no additional text beyond the jersey sponsor, bat branding, small kit marks, and the three angle labels.
